title: Provider Capability
summary: Generated reader for the C3 Provider Capability domain: runtime capability snapshot model, protocol vocabulary, compatibility summary values, fingerprint input shape, and protocol-critical args.
invariants: `ProtocolKind` is the primary adapter-selection key; `ProviderKind` alone is insufficient.
`DetectedVersion` is raw signal only; runtime branching uses protocol and surface facts.
`CompatibilityStatus` is a lossy summary; gate decisions must also read maturity, opt-in, missing capability, blocked, and degraded reason fields.
`ExposesUnsafePermissionBypass` is a risk signal, not permission to enable bypass execution.
Event stream facts are provider-neutral: `SupportsStructuredEventStream` plus `EventStreamFormat`.
`CapabilityFingerprint` excludes `NativeConfigVersion`; native config is task execution context, not runtime capability.
Unknown provider surfaces are preserved instead of being dropped.
Protocol-critical CLI args are owned by `provider/capability.ProtocolCriticalArgs`.

loop.observation: Provider capability had an executable package and tests, but the public reader was a long hand-maintained SSOT.
loop.hypothesis: Generating the reader from the package catalog and reflective model counts will keep protocol/fingerprint governance aligned with code.
loop.execute: Load provider/capability catalogs, verify expected counts, render the reader, and upload provider-capability evidence in CI.
loop.evaluate: The verifier fails on stale markdown, malformed manifest, workflow gaps, package shape drift, or protocol-critical arg catalog drift.
loop.retrospective: Provider capability knowledge becomes executable while the rich details stay in package APIs and tests.
